articles.title: Arizona Charges Kalshi With Illegal Gambling Operation
articles.summary: Arizona regulators hit Kalshi with charges for allegedly running an illegal gambling operation in the state. The case could set a precedent for how other states treat prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to navigate a patchwork of gambling laws or face similar crackdowns.

articles.title: Polymarket let people bet on when Iran would strike Israel. A journalist who covered the war got death threats.
articles.summary: Polymarket users wagered on the timing of Iran's retaliation against Israel, and a war correspondent covering the conflict received death threats afterward. The incident highlights how prediction markets on geopolitical violence can create perverse incentives and put journalists at risk when their reporting affects bet outcomes.

articles.title: Betting on the Oscars or the Super Bowl halftime show would be banned under new prediction market bill
articles.summary: A new bill would ban prediction markets from offering bets on entertainment events like the Oscars and Super Bowl halftime shows. The restriction signals lawmakers want to draw clearer lines between "legitimate" forecasting on policy outcomes and what they view as frivolous gambling on pop culture.

articles.title: Major prediction market faces 34th country ban
articles.summary: Another country has banned a major prediction market platform, bringing the total number of jurisdictions blocking access to 34. The growing regulatory crackdown signals that governments view prediction markets as gambling rather than information aggregation tools, forcing platforms to choose between compliance costs and global reach.

articles.title: U.S. Democratic lawmakers introduce bill to crack down on prediction markets
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers want to restrict prediction markets, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket that have exploded in popularity during election season. If it gains traction, this could force major platforms to delist political markets or face regulatory crackdowns, potentially pushing the action back to offshore sites where U.S. bettors already wager billions.

articles.title: Betting on 5-minute swings on Bitcoin price are the hot new thing on prediction markets
articles.summary: Traders are now placing bets on whether Bitcoin will move up or down in just 5-minute windows, turning crypto prediction markets into something that resembles high-frequency day trading. This ultra-short timeframe betting represents prediction markets evolving from long-term forecasting tools into real-time gambling products that could attract millions of new users seeking instant gratification.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Split on Next U.S. President: See Latest Odds for 2028 Election
articles.summary: Prediction markets are already pricing the 2028 presidential race with notable disagreement between platforms on who leads the field. The early splits signal either major information asymmetries between market makers or genuine uncertainty about a wide-open race that could create profitable arbitrage opportunities for sharp bettors.

articles.title: Make the Predictions Come True
articles.summary: Some prediction markets are shifting from pure forecasting to actively trying to influence the outcomes they're betting on. This blurs the line between predicting and manipulating events, which could either supercharge market accuracy or completely undermine their credibility as information sources.

articles.title: Perfection is nearly impossible: Kalshi is giving away $1 billion for a perfect bracket
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $1 billion to anyone who can correctly predict every game in March Madness, joining the long tradition of impossible bracket challenges that generate massive publicity while costing almost nothing. The stunt shows how prediction markets are using lottery-style promotions to attract mainstream attention, even though the mathematical odds make Warren Buffett's old $1 billion bracket bet look generous.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket correctly predicted 19 out of 24 Oscar winners. Here are the ones they missed.
articles.summary: Prediction markets Kalshi and Polymarket nailed 79% of Oscar winners this year, missing only 5 categories out of 24. The strong performance adds more credibility to betting markets as entertainment forecasting tools, especially as Hollywood awards become increasingly predictable through industry sentiment and campaign spending.

articles.title: Why Does The CFTC Chair Want People To Bet On Drug Trials At Prediction Markets?
articles.summary: CFTC Chair Rostin Behnam is pushing for prediction markets that let people bet on pharmaceutical drug trial outcomes. If regulators green-light pharma betting, it could unlock billions in trading volume while giving investors real-time odds on which drugs will actually make it to market.

articles.title: The Polymarket Gamblers Betting on the Value of Your Apartment
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are now placing bets on real estate prices, turning your local housing market into another prediction market playground. This signals prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into everyday economic indicators that actually affect people's wallets, creating new opportunities for informed bettors to profit from housing knowledge.
